strategy: Death boss that resists Death and Ice 80%, so hit with Fire or Life (each +10%) and never attack into Death or Ice. He cannot be stunned.
Feint gets Swap Warded straight back onto you, so protect it with Indemnity, dispel with Melt, or skip Feint and stack blades instead.
Do not heal in the open: any heal, pets included, triggers Doom and Gloom. Bring Strangle to dispel it or top up before it lands.

The Fairy Queen summons two 1,750-health Fairies on Round 1 and every 4 rounds after a minion dies, and can Enfeeble the wizard who blades or casts a global, so spread setup out or Dissipate the Enfeeble.
Funnel blades to one hitter, keep the Fairy adds in check, then land one big Fire or Life hit.
cheatNotes: Join before combat starts or the latecomer gets Mana Burned.
You can Unbalance to dispel that Mana Burn.
If Rattlebones is already down, the Fairy Queen throws the Mana Burn instead.
Any healing spell makes him drop Doom and Gloom to choke your heals.
Strangle removes that Doom and Gloom.
Even pet may-cast heals set off the Doom and Gloom.
Feint him and he Swap Wards it, moving the bigger trap onto you, a bad trade.
Melt can dispel the swapped-back trap.
Indemnity stops the Feint swap entirely, the cleanest fix.
The Fairy Queen summons two random-school 1,750-health Fairies on Round 1 and again every 4 rounds after a minion dies.
Entangle will not stop the summon, it just delays the Fairies going active by two rounds.
Blading or casting a global can draw an Enfeeble onto that wizard from the Queen.
With a mass blade like Bladestorm she only Enfeebles the caster, not the whole team.
Feint on the Fairy Queen gets swapped back the same way, so protect or skip it there too.
